differential_diagnoses: Acute viral encephalitis (e.g., HSV or HHV-6)
Delirium due to occult substance toxicity (e.g., sympathomimetic)
next_steps: Obtain emergent thyroid function tests (TSH, Free T4, T3) and TSH-receptor antibody (TRAb) level.
Order a comprehensive autoimmune/paraneoplastic encephalitis panel for serum and CSF (specifically for NMDA receptor antibodies).
Initiate continuous EEG monitoring to rule out ongoing non-convulsive status epilepticus.
Obtain MRI Brain with contrast (protocol optimized for encephalitis/vasculitis) if current imaging quality remains insufficient.
Check serum ammonia, ceruloplasmin, and heavy metal screening.
Consider early empiric intravenous immunoglobulin (IVIG) or high-dose corticosteroids if encephalitis is highly suspected.
Stabilize hemodynamics with continuous cardiac monitoring and cautious use of beta-blockers, ensuring metabolic/endocrine parameters are assessed first.
